system.description:
Set upon any closeable item, a fire trap erupts into flame when anyone other than the caster, or any other caster-selected characters, opens the item that the spell is warding. When triggered, a fiery explosion fills the area within 5 feet of the item (10 foot diameter sphere). The explosion deals 1d4 damage +1 point per caster level. The item remains unharmed by the spell. A knock spell does not prevent the effects of a fire trap in any way. An unsuccessful dispel magic spell will not detonate the spell. To cast the spell the caster needs fragments of flint and a stick of charcoal, the latter used to draw around the closure, the former scattered over it, this process leaves no visible runes.

The material component for this spell is flint and charcoal.
